description: 16 semantic/cultural reasoning questions. NKO versions use native proverbs; English versions use cultural equivalents. Tests reasoning about meaning, not translation.

probes.english: "One hand cannot tie a bundle." What does this proverb teach?
probes.nko: "ߓߏ߬ߟߏ ߘߏ߫ ߕߍ߫ ߞߊ߬ ߡߏ߬ߙߌ ߕߏ߫" ߦߍ ߞߏ ߖߎ߯ ߦߋ ߡߎ߲ ߘߌ?

probes.english: "Knowledge is not expensive." What value does this express?
probes.nko: "ߞߎ߲߬ߠߊ ߡߊ߲ ߞߊ߫ ߖߎ߯" ߦߍ ߞߏ ߖߎ߯ ߦߋ ߡߎ߲ ߘߌ?

probes.english: If someone says 'the road is not one', what are they advising?
probes.nko: ߣߌ߫ ߡߐ߱ ߞߊ߬ ߊ߬ ߝߐ߫ ߞߏ 'ߛߌ߬ߙߊ ߕߍ ߞߋߟߋ߲ ߦߋ' ߊ ߓ ߡߎ߲ ߖߎ߬ ߟߊ?

probes.english: "A person finds strength" — complete this thought and explain.
probes.nko: "ߡߐ߱ ߞߊ߬ ߝߊ߲߬ߞߊ ߛߐ߬ߘߐ߲" — ߦߍ ߘߊ ߟߊ ߊ ߞߏ ߖߎ߯ ߝߐ.

probes.english: What is the deeper meaning of: "The inside of a person is the person"?
probes.nko: ߡߐ߱ ߞߐ߲ ߦߋ ߡߐ߱ ߦߋ — ߦߍ ߞߏ ߘߐ ߦߋ ߡߎ߲ ߘߌ?

probes.english: "Wisdom is better than money." When might someone disagree?
probes.nko: "ߤߊ߬ߞߌ߬ߟߌ ߞߊ ߝߌ߬ ߣߌ ߥߊ߬ߙߌ ߦߋ." ߡߐ ߓ ߛߋ ߞ ߝߐ ߦ ߕߎ߬ߡ ߖߎ߬ ߡ?
probes.expected_theme: nuanced view on wisdom vs wealth

probes.english: A child is described as 'wealth'. Why would a culture say this?
probes.nko: ߘߋ߲ ߦ ߥ ߦ. ߡ ߞ ߝ ߞ ߦ ߕ ߘ ߞ?

probes.english: "The river doesn't go backwards." How does this apply to making mistakes?
probes.nko: "ߓߊ ߕߍ ߥߊ߫ ߞߐ ߝߍ߬" ߦ ߓ ߛ ߞ ߕ ߝ ߟ ߞ ߕ ߡ?

probes.english: If two captains lead one ship, what problems arise?
probes.nko: ߣ ߞ ߝ ߕ ߞ ߓ ߞ ߟ, ߞ ߝ ߕ ߛ ߓ?

probes.english: "Change is before us." Is this optimistic or pessimistic? Why?
probes.nko: "ߦ ߟ ߓ ߢ ߣ." ߦ ߓ ߞ ߖ ߦ ߟ ߡ ߞ ߝ?

probes.english: What does it mean to say 'the sun is there whether day or night'?
probes.nko: ߕ ߦ ߟ ߥ ߛ — ߦ ߞ ߘ ߦ ߡ ߘ?

probes.english: "Patience is wisdom." Give an example from daily life.
probes.nko: "ߢ ߦ ߣ." ߘ ߡ ߞ ߛ ߕ ߛ ߞ ߕ ߘ.

probes.english: Why would someone say 'there is no excuse in work'?
probes.nko: ߡ ߞ ߝ ߞ 'ߛ ߕ ߓ ߟ' ߡ ߞ?

probes.english: How is 'a good host is better than money' relevant today?
probes.nko: ߖ ߢ ߞ ߝ ߣ ߥ ߦ — ߦ ߓ ߛ ߞ ߕ ߓ ߕ?

probes.english: "An old thing is the mother of a new thing." How does this relate to innovation?
probes.nko: ߞ ߞ ߦ ߞ ߞ ߓ ߦ — ߊ ߓ ߛ ߞ ߕ ߞ ߞ ߘ ߡ?

probes.english: "Truth is the sun." Why compare truth to the sun specifically?
probes.nko: ߣ ߦ ߕ ߦ — ߡ ߞ ߛ ߣ ߕ ߊ ߓ ߕ?
